title: SSH Certificate Authority in known_hosts blocks podman
body:
### Issue Description

If an SSH Certificate Authority is configured in .ssh/known_hosts to validate the authenticity of a host, it blocks podman from executing.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Insert an [SSH Certificate Authority](https://man.openbsd.org/ssh-keygen#CERTIFICATES) into ~/.ssh/known_hosts
`@cert-authority * ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIL0r3m1p5UmD0L0rS1t4m3tC0n53c7e7Ur4D1p15c1n6 Lorem Ipsum` 
2. Initialise a Podman Machine
`podman machine init`
3. Start Podman Machine
`podman machine start`
4. Attempt a podman command that communicates with the podman machine instance
`podman system info`


### Describe the results you received

Podman exits with the following error
`podman --log-level debug system info`

```
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug       
DEBU[0000] Called info.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level debug system info)
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "/Users/username/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/machine" SHA256:t6Ol1r4g+5hhhDO4E3Zr6LnMARNhXywEz6zLx/58ngY ssh-ed25519 
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: ssh: no common algorithm for host key; we offered: [ssh-ed25519-cert-v01@openssh.com], peer offered: [rsa-sha2-512 rsa-sha2-256 ecdsa-sha2-nistp256 ssh-ed25519]
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
```

### Describe the results you expected

The podman command should execute without error

`podman --log-level debug ps -a`

```
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level debug ps -a) 
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "/Users/username/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/machine" SHA256:t6Ol1r4g+5hhhDO4E3Zr6LnMARNhXywEz6zLx/58ngY ssh-ed25519 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v5.7.1/libpod/_ping 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v5.7.1/libpod/containers/json 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPostRunE(podman --log-level debug ps -a) 
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

`sw_vers`

```
ProductName:		macOS
ProductVersion:		15.7.3
BuildVersion:		24G419
```


### Additional information

The issue can be mitigated if the SSH Certificate Authority is restricted to be more restrictive than the catch-all of *

Catch-All:
`@cert-authority * ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIL0r3m1p5UmD0L0rS1t4m3tC0n53c7e7Ur4D1p15c1n6 Lorem Ipsum` 
Limited to *.localdomain:
`@cert-authority *.localdomain ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIL0r3m1p5UmD0L0rS1t4m3tC0n53c7e7Ur4D1p15c1n6 Lorem Ipsum` 


This issue does not interfere with Podman Remote connections over ssh, to check the remote host against the SSH Certificate Authority and execute remote podman commands without issues.   
`podman --connection $remote system info`
runs without issues

The issue affects the local podman machine running on the locally.
`podman --connection podman-machine-default system info`
would error out.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
